page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter Age Policy: Minimum Ages for Guests & Skippers

data.meta_description: Learn the marbella yacht charter age policy: minimum age for guests is 5, skippers must be 18+. Clear rules for your Marbella boat rental.
data.summary: Understand the marbella yacht charter age policy: guests must be at least 5 years old, and skippers must be 18+. This guide covers age limits for all our boats departing from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Minimum guest age for charter: 5 years old.
Skipper must be at least 18 years old with a valid license.
All charters include a licensed skipper – no self-drive option.
Infants under 5 cannot be accommodated due to safety regulations.
No upper age limit for guests, but mobility may affect boarding.
All boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Pricing includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.

faq.q: What is the minimum age for guests on a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: The minimum age for guests on our charters is 5 years old. This applies to all boats in our fleet, including the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39. Infants under 5 cannot be accommodated due to safety regulations and life jacket sizing.

faq.q: Is there a minimum age for the skipper on a Marbella yacht charter?
faq.a: Yes, the skipper must be at least 18 years old and hold a valid boating license. All our charters include a licensed skipper, so you do not need to drive the boat yourself. The skipper is responsible for navigation and safety.

faq.q: Can I drive the boat myself if I am under 18?
faq.a: No, you cannot drive the boat yourself regardless of age. All our charters include a licensed skipper who is at least 18 years old. This ensures safety and compliance with local maritime regulations in Marbella.

faq.q: Are there any age restrictions for the jet ski included with the Mangusta 80?
faq.a: Yes, the jet ski included with the Mangusta 80 (and its white/grey variants) has a minimum age requirement of 16 years old to operate. Guests under 16 can ride as passengers with a responsible adult. This is standard for safety.

faq.q: What is the maximum number of guests allowed on a charter?
faq.a: The maximum guest capacity varies by boat. For example, the Azimut 58 Flybridge and Mangusta 80 accommodate up to 12 guests, while the Dubhe holds 5. Our age policy applies to all guests, regardless of boat size. Check the fleet page for specifics.

faq.q: Do you allow children under 5 on any boats?
faq.a: No, we do not allow children under 5 on any of our charters. This is a strict policy due to safety equipment sizing and emergency procedures. We recommend waiting until your child is at least 5 years old to enjoy a boat rental in Marbella.

faq.q: Is there an upper age limit for guests on a yacht charter?
faq.a: There is no upper age limit for guests. However, boarding some boats may require stepping onto the vessel from the dock, which could be challenging for those with mobility issues. Please contact us via WhatsApp to discuss specific needs.

faq.q: What documents do I need to provide for age verification?
faq.a: We require a valid passport or national ID for all guests and the skipper. This is used for age verification and compliance with maritime authority requirements in Puerto Banús. No other documentation is needed for the charter itself.

data.body_html:
Planning a boat rental in Marbella? Understanding the marbella yacht charter age policy is essential for a smooth experience. Whether you're booking the Astondoa 40 for a family day out or the Mangusta 80 for a group celebration, age rules apply to both guests and skippers. This guide covers minimum ages, safety requirements, and what to expect when chartering from Puerto Banús.

Minimum Age for Guests

All guests must be at least 5 years old to join any of our charters. This applies to every boat in our fleet, from the compact Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) to the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax). Infants under 5 cannot be accommodated due to safety regulations, including life jacket sizing and emergency evacuation procedures. If you have younger children, we recommend waiting until they reach the minimum age to enjoy a day on the Costa del Sol.

Skipper Age Requirements

Every charter includes a licensed skipper who must be at least 18 years old. The skipper holds a valid Spanish or international boating license and is responsible for navigation, safety, and compliance with local maritime laws. You do not need a license yourself; the skipper handles all operations. This ensures a relaxed experience, whether you're cruising to Cala del Faro or anchoring near Cabopino.

No Self-Drive Option

We do not offer bareboat or self-drive charters. All bookings include a professional skipper. This policy simplifies your planning—no need to worry about licenses or local regulations. It also enhances safety, especially for groups with mixed ages. The skipper can adjust the itinerary based on weather and guest preferences, from the Golden Mile to Estepona.

Age Policy for Jet Skis

If you book a Mangusta 80 (including white or grey variants), a jet ski is included free for minimum 4-hour charters. The minimum age to operate the jet ski is 16 years old. Guests under 16 can ride as passengers with a responsible adult. This policy aligns with local safety standards in Puerto Banús. Other boats do not include jet skis, but you can inquire about add-ons via WhatsApp.

Guest Capacity by Boat

Maximum guest numbers vary by vessel, but the age policy remains consistent. For example:

  • Astondoa 40: 9 pax
  • Azimut 39: 11 pax
  • Mangusta 80: 12 pax
  • Dubhe: 5 pax
  • Speedboat: 6 pax

All guests count toward the capacity, including children. Overloading is not permitted for safety reasons. Choose a boat that fits your group size from our fleet.

Upper Age Limit and Accessibility

There is no upper age limit for guests. However, boarding some boats may require stepping from the dock onto the vessel, which could be challenging for those with mobility issues. Our team can advise on the easiest boats to board, such as the Azimut 58 Flybridge with its wider gangway. Contact us via WhatsApp to discuss specific needs before booking.

Documentation Required

For age verification, all guests and the skipper must provide a valid passport or national ID. This is a standard requirement for all charters departing from Puerto Banús. No other documentation is needed. We handle all paperwork with the local maritime authorities, so you can focus on enjoying your day on the water.

What's Included in Every Charter

All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), pricing starts at €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) start at €4,719 for 4 hours. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum age for guests on a yacht charter in Marbella?

The minimum age for guests on our charters is 5 years old. This applies to all boats in our fleet, including the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39. Infants under 5 cannot be accommodated due to safety regulations and life jacket sizing.

Is there a minimum age for the skipper on a Marbella yacht charter?

Yes, the skipper must be at least 18 years old and hold a valid boating license. All our charters include a licensed skipper, so you do not need to drive the boat yourself. The skipper is responsible for navigation and safety.

Can I drive the boat myself if I am under 18?

No, you cannot drive the boat yourself regardless of age. All our charters include a licensed skipper who is at least 18 years old. This ensures safety and compliance with local maritime regulations in Marbella.

Are there any age restrictions for the jet ski included with the Mangusta 80?

Yes, the jet ski included with the Mangusta 80 (and its white/grey variants) has a minimum age requirement of 16 years old to operate. Guests under 16 can ride as passengers with a responsible adult. This is standard for safety.

What is the maximum number of guests allowed on a charter?

The maximum guest capacity varies by boat. For example, the Azimut 58 Flybridge and Mangusta 80 accommodate up to 12 guests, while the Dubhe holds 5. Our age policy applies to all guests, regardless of boat size. Check the fleet page for specifics.

Do you allow children under 5 on any boats?

No, we do not allow children under 5 on any of our charters. This is a strict policy due to safety equipment sizing and emergency procedures. We recommend waiting until your child is at least 5 years old to enjoy a boat rental in Marbella.

Is there an upper age limit for guests on a yacht charter?

There is no upper age limit for guests. However, boarding some boats may require stepping onto the vessel from the dock, which could be challenging for those with mobility issues. Please contact us via WhatsApp to discuss specific needs.

What documents do I need to provide for age verification?

We require a valid passport or national ID for all guests and the skipper. This is used for age verification and compliance with maritime authority requirements in Puerto Banús. No other documentation is needed for the charter itself.
